title: Moroccan Date Tagine
description: A traditional North African stew made with sweet dates and spices, typically cooked in a clay pot with a conical lid. This recipe cooks the tagine with the addition of gooey cheese for an extra rich flavor.
instructions: Heat oil in the bottom of a clay tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the dates, spice blend, salt, and almonds. Stir to combine.
Add the gooey cheese and stir until melted and smooth.
Transfer the tagine to the oven and bake at 300°F (150°C) for 20-25 minutes.
Garnish with parsley and serve hot over couscous.
